view.s2056.qtip.election: Executor must affirmatively elect QTIP on Form 706 within 9 mo + 6 mo extension
view.s2056.qtip.income_interest: Surviving spouse must receive ALL income annually for life
view.s2056.qtip.income_required: Income must be distributed at LEAST annually
view.s2056.qtip.no_gst: QTIP DOES NOT use GST exemption — separate election needed
view.s2056.qtip.no_others: No other person may receive income or principal during spouse's life
view.s2056.qtip.partial: Partial election allowed (only portion of trust qualifies)
view.s2056.qtip.reverse: Reverse QTIP election for GST: partial elect on portion for QTIP, reverse on rest
view.s2056.qtip.spouse_estate: QTIP property INCLUDED in surviving spouse's estate at death (§ 2044)
view.s2056.warning.non_citizen: Non-citizen spouse: marital deduction DENIED on outright transfers. Use QDOT trust. $185k (2024) annual exclusion for inter-spouse gifts during life; death-time transfers ONLY through QDOT.
view.s213.h1.title: // § 213 MEDICAL EXPENSE DEDUCTION
view.s213.hint.intro: Itemize only. Deduct medical / dental expenses > 7.5% of AGI. Spouse + dependent expenses count. LTC services + LTC premiums (age-limited) qualify. Capital expenditures for medical necessity (wheelchair ramp, lift, custom kitchen) also count. NOT deductible: cosmetic, gym memberships, vitamins, HSA-paid amounts (no double-dip), employer-reimbursed.
view.s213.qual.capital: Capital expenditures (wheelchair ramp, stair lift, modified vehicle) — reduce by increase in property value
view.s213.qual.doctors: Doctors, dentists, surgeons, chiropractors, psychiatrists, optometrists, podiatrists
view.s213.qual.special_school: Special education for learning disabilities (medical necessity)
view.s219.ap.401k: Eligible to participate in employer 401(k) / 403(b) / 457(b) — even if no contributions made
view.s219.ap.contributions: Received employer contributions during year (SEP, SIMPLE)
view.s219.ap.exclusions: NOT: § 457(b) gov't only (after 2007), pure deferred comp, 401(k) you joined late in year
view.s219.ap.federal: Federal Civil Service Retirement System / FERS
view.s219.dl.april_15: April 15 (or tax day) of following year for prior-year contribution
view.s219.dl.excess_removal: Excess contribution removal by Oct 15 (extended) avoids 6% penalty
view.s219.dl.no_extension: Extension to file does NOT extend contribution deadline
view.s219.dl.recharacterization: Roth conversion CANNOT be recharacterized (TCJA repeal)
view.s219.h1.title: // § 219 IRA DEDUCTION
view.s219.hint.intro: 2024: $7,000 base ($8,000 if 50+). Phase-out if active participant in employer plan: Single: $77k-$87k. MFJ (both active): $123k-$143k. MFJ (spouse active): $230k-$240k. No income limit if neither spouse covered by employer plan. Spousal IRA: working spouse covers non-working with own earned income. Roth (§ 408A) has different MAGI limits + no deduction.
view.s219.nd.50_penalty: Form 8606 failure to file: $50 penalty per year (Form 8606 standalone if no return)
view.s219.nd.cost_basis: Future Roth conversion: pro-rata between basis + earnings
view.s219.nd.form_8606: File Form 8606 every year to track basis (lifetime!)
view.s219.nd.pro_rata: Backdoor Roth: pro-rata rule applies if pre-tax IRA balance exists
view.s219.nd.spousal_separate: Spousal basis tracked separately on each return
view.s221.h1.title: // § 221 STUDENT LOAN INTEREST
view.s221.hint.intro: Above-the-line deduction (Schedule 1) up to $2,500/year per RETURN. MAGI phase-out 2024: $80k-$95k single / $165k-$195k MFJ. MFS NOT allowed. Cannot be claimed if you're a DEPENDENT on someone else's return. Loan must be qualified — for taxpayer / spouse / dependent's education. Voluntary and capitalized interest both count.
view.s221.label.dependent: Claimed as dependent on another return?
view.s221.note.secure: Effective 2024: employers can MATCH employee student loan payments to 401(k) / 403(b) plan. Treated like deferral for match purposes — even if employee makes no plan contribution. Helps young workers debt-paydown + retirement save simultaneously.
view.s221.qual.education: Money used SOLELY to pay qualified education expenses
view.s221.qual.refinanced: Refinanced student loans: maintain qualified character if proceeds used only to pay original
view.s221.qual.related: NOT from related person (parent), employer benefit, or qualified employer plan
view.s221.qual.timing: Loan must be primarily liable on (cosigners may share)
view.s221.qual.who: For taxpayer / spouse / dependent at time loan taken out
view.s221.warning.ineligible: Not eligible: MFS filers + dependents on another return are disqualified.
view.s23.h1.title: // § 23 ADOPTION CREDIT
view.s23.hint.intro: Up to $16,810 (2024) credit per child for qualified adoption expenses. MAGI phase-out: $252,150-$292,150 (2024). 5-yr carryforward (non-refundable). Special needs adoption: FULL credit regardless of expenses incurred. § 137: employer-paid adoption assistance up to $16,810 excluded from income (separate, NOT double-dip). Form 8839.
view.s23.qual.illegal: NOT: expenses violating state law / public policy
view.s23.qual.reimbursed: NOT: expenses reimbursed by employer / charity / gov't program
view.s23.qual.travel: Travel + lodging + meals (while traveling for adoption)
view.s23.rel.actc: § 24 Additional CTC: refundable portion up to $1,700 (2024)
view.s23.rel.cdcc: § 21 Child + Dependent Care Credit: 20-35% on up to $3k/$6k expenses
view.s23.rel.ctc: § 24 Child Tax Credit: $2,000 per qualifying child < 17
view.s23.sn.factors: Factors: ethnic background, age, sibling group, medical/emotional condition
view.s23.sn.harder_to_place: State determines specific factor makes child harder to place
view.s23.sn.state_determined: State determines child cannot return to parents' home
view.s23.sn.tax_id: Use Adoption Taxpayer ID Number (ATIN) before SSN issued
view.s23.sn.title_iv_e: Title IV-E foster care payments don't reduce credit
view.s23.sn.us_citizen: US citizen or resident at time adoption efforts began
view.s24.h1.title: // § 24 CHILD TAX CREDIT
view.s24.hint.intro: 2024 CTC $2,000/child under 17, $1,700 refundable ACTC. $500 ODC for other deps. MAGI phase $200K/$400K. SSN required. Sunsets to $1K post-2025.
view.s245A.h1.title: // § 245A 100% DRD (Participation)
view.s245A.hint.intro: 100% deduction for foreign-source portion of dividends from 10%-owned foreign corp ('specified 10% owned'). Holding period: 365 / 731 days (45 / 90 for preferred). Hybrid dividend rule (§ 245A(e)): deduction DENIED if foreign payor deducted it. § 1248 gain on CFC stock sale recharacterized to E&P → eligible for § 245A. Net effect: US C-corps largely exempt from tax on actual foreign dividends.
view.s245A.req.holding: Holding period: 365 days within 731-day window (45 / 90 days for preferred)
view.s245A.req.no_deduction: Expenses allocable to deductible dividend NOT deductible (no whipsaw)
view.s245A.req.no_ftc: No § 901 FTC + no § 902 indirect credit on excluded portion (logical — no income to credit against)
view.s245A.req.no_hybrid: Not a hybrid dividend (would create double non-tax)
view.s245A.req.not_pfic: Foreign payor cannot be PFIC for any year of US shareholder ownership
view.s245A.req.specified10: 'Specified 10% owned' foreign corp: 10%+ by vote OR value
view.s245A.req.us_corp: Recipient must be US C-corp (no S-corp, no individual)
view.s245A.s1248.basic: CFC stock sale: gain up to allocated E&P recharacterized as DIVIDEND under § 1248
view.s245A.s1248.deconsolidation: Trumps gain on CFC sale → enables tax-free repatriation via sale
view.s245A.s1248.deduction: Recharacterized portion eligible for § 245A 100% DRD (huge planning win)
view.s245A.s1248.requires: Still requires holding period + foreign source + not hybrid
view.s245A.s1248_note: § 1248 recharacterized gain captured under § 245A — full DRD on E&P portion. Net result: tax-free repatriation via CFC sale. Coordinate with § 367 outbound transfer rules.
view.s248.elect.amortization_begin: Amortization begins month business begins (active business start)
view.s248.elect.default: DEFAULT election: deemed elected if return reports per § 248 treatment
view.s248.elect.election_capitalize: Election to CAPITALIZE entire amount (no current deduction): irrevocable
view.s248.elect.no_form: No separate election form — choice made on first return
view.s248.elect.short_period: Short first year: prorated based on months
view.s248.eligible.accounting: Accounting fees: setting up books + records + first audit
view.s248.eligible.federal_filing: Federal filing fees: EIN, S-corp Form 2553, exempt org Form 1023
view.s248.eligible.legal: Legal fees: incorporation, drafting articles + bylaws, organizing meetings
view.s248.eligible.organizational_meetin: Organizational meetings + temporary directors / officers compensation
view.s248.eligible.printing: Printing of stock certificates, corporate seals, minutes book
view.s248.eligible.registered_agent: Registered agent / resident agent setup fees
view.s248.eligible.state_filing: State filing fees: Articles of Incorporation, Statement of Information
view.s248.h1.title: // § 248 ORGANIZATIONAL EXPENDITURES
view.s248.hint.intro: $5K immediate deduction in first tax year + remaining amortized over 180 months (15 yrs). Phaseout: $5K reduces $-for-$ when org exp > $50K → no immediate at $55K. Parallel: § 195 startup + § 709 partnership organizational. Eligible expenses: legal fees, accounting fees, state filing fees, incorporation costs. NOT eligible: stock issuance costs, transfer of assets, lobbying. Election: made on first return; no separate form (Reg § 1.248-1).
view.s248.ineligible.stock_issuance: Stock issuance costs: § 263(a) capital expenditure — reduce stock proceeds
view.s248.ineligible.transfer_assets: Transfer of assets to new corp: separate § 351 / § 1032 treatment
view.s248.phaseout_note: Organizational expenditures EXCEED $55K phaseout — NO immediate $5K deduction. Entire amount must be amortized over 180 months. Common for sophisticated corporate structures with extensive legal, accounting, securities counsel work. Consider whether to fully capitalize to basis instead (election made on first return).
view.s250.fdii.deemed: Deemed Intangible Income = DEI − 10% × Domestic QBAI
view.s250.fdii.dei: DEI = Deduction Eligible Income (gross income less subF, GILTI, dividends, etc.)
view.s250.fdii.documentation: Documentation required: sales contracts + delivery proof + foreign-use evidence
view.s250.fdii.fdii: FDII = (DEII / DEI) × DEII (Foreign-Derived Income from Eligible Use)
view.s250.fdii.foreign_pct: Foreign-use fraction = sales to foreign / total sales (services or sales)
view.s250.fdii.related_party: Related-party sales to non-US end user qualifies if documented to ultimate use
view.s250.h1.title: // § 250 FDII + GILTI DEDUCTION
view.s250.hint.intro: FDII = 37.5% deduction → ~13.125% effective rate (TCJA carrot to keep IP in US). GILTI = 50% deduction → ~10.5% effective rate (anti-deferral counterweight). Post-2025: FDII drops to 21.875% (16.4% effective); GILTI drops to 37.5% (13.125% effective). Limitation: deduction ≤ taxable income (no NOL). C-corps only (and § 962-electing individuals).
view.s250.qual.commodities: Commodity trading + financial product trading excluded
view.s250.qual.goods: Tangible property sold for foreign use (manufactured + customized + warranty)
view.s250.qual.intangibles: Licenses + royalties on IP used outside US
view.s250.qual.related_us: NOT qualifying: sales to foreign related party for US-end use
view.s250.qual.services: Services performed FOR foreign person (delivery point matters)
view.s250.ti_cap_note: Taxable income limitation triggered: full uncapped deduction exceeds TI. Deduction reduced to prevent NOL creation. Unused portion does NOT carry forward — lost permanently.
view.s2518.fail.cost_basis: Disclaimant becomes property owner — § 1014 step-up still applies
view.s2518.fail.deemed_gift: Treated as accepted + new gift from disclaimant to next beneficiary
view.s2518.fail.gift_tax: § 2511 gift tax applies — file Form 709 + use lifetime exemption
view.s2518.fail.late_filing: 9-month deadline is strict — no equitable tolling
view.s2518.fail.partial_consequences: Partial disclaimer with conditions = total failure (not partial)
view.s2518.fail.state_law_only: State-law disclaimer may still effective for state purposes
view.s2518.failed_note: Disclaimer FAILS § 2518 — treated as accepted + gift from disclaimant. Triggers § 2511 gift tax. State-law disclaimer may still effective for non-tax purposes.
view.s2518.five.passes: Passes WITHOUT disclaimant direction (governing instrument controls)
view.s2518.five.timing: 9 months from later of: (a) date of transfer creating interest, OR (b) age 21 for minor
view.s2518.five.unconditional: UNCONDITIONAL + irrevocable — no conditions, no power to revoke
view.s2518.five.written: WRITTEN refusal — signed by disclaimant or legal rep
view.s2518.h1.title: // § 2518 QUALIFIED DISCLAIMER
view.s2518.hint.intro: Qualified disclaimer = refusal to accept inheritance. 5 reqs: written + 9 months + unconditional + no benefit + passes without disclaimant direction. NOT a gift under § 2511.
view.s2518.special.fmv_date: FMV at date of disclaimer (not date of original transfer)
view.s2518.special.formula: Formula clause disclaimer permitted (e.g., applicable exclusion)
view.s2518.special.ira_separate: IRA / retirement: each beneficiary disclaims separately
view.s2518.special.partial_undivided: Partial disclaimer: must be UNDIVIDED interest (severable share)
view.s2518.special.power_of_appointment: General power of appointment may be disclaimed
view.s2518.special.spouse_exception: Otherwise, disclaimed interest CANNOT pass to disclaimant
view.s2518.special.spouse_redirect: § 2518(b)(4)(A) — surviving spouse may disclaim AND interest passes to spouse
view.s2518.state.adoption: Adopted in 40+ states (CA, NY, IL, TX vary)
view.s2518.state.no_time_limit: UDPIA: NO state law time limit (federal 9-month controls for tax)
view.s2518.state.udpia: Uniform Disclaimer of Property Interests Act (UDPIA, 2002)
view.s2518.state.unborn: Unborn beneficiary: virtual representation may apply
view.s2518.uc.bypass_trust: Disclaimer to bypass trust funding (reduces surviving spouse's estate)
view.s2518.uc.creditor: Creditor protection: disclaimed property never owned by disclaimant
view.s2518.uc.elderly: Elderly child / financially independent — disclaim in favor of next gen
view.s2518.uc.poor_planning: Post-mortem correction of pre-mortem planning errors
view.s2518.uc.qtip_election: Disclaim to enable QTIP election + reverse direction
view.s2518.uc.skip_generation: Disclaimer to skip generation (children disclaim → grandchildren — GST issues)
view.s25D.carry_note: Unused credit carries forward INDEFINITELY — Roth conversion, IRA distribution, or other taxable event can use credit. § 25D is non-refundable but never expires. Unlike § 38 GBC (20-yr limit), § 25D has no time limit on carryforward.
view.s25D.elig.battery_storage: Battery storage ≥ 3 kWh — added IRA 2022 as standalone (no solar pairing required)
view.s25D.elig.financing: Financing costs (interest) NOT in basis; deductible separately
view.s25D.elig.fuel_cell: Fuel cell: $500/0.5 kW capacity limit (max $1,667 / household per yr × 30% = $500)
view.s25D.elig.geothermal: Geothermal heat pump: Energy Star certified; loop + heat exchanger + pump
view.s25D.elig.labor: Labor + installation INCLUDED in credit basis
view.s25D.elig.roof: NOT eligible: structural roof reinforcement, general roof replacement (unless integrated PV)
view.s25D.elig.solar_pv: Solar PV: panels, inverters, wiring, mounting, installation labor
view.s25D.elig.solar_thermal: Solar thermal water heating: NOT pool / hot tub heating
view.s25D.elig.wind: Small wind: ≤ 100 kW capacity, residential property
view.s25D.h1.title: // § 25D RESIDENTIAL CLEAN ENERGY
view.s25D.hint.intro: 30% credit on residential property: solar PV, solar thermal water, fuel cells, geothermal heat pump, small wind, battery storage ≥ 3 kWh. IRA 2022 extended through 2034 (26% in 2033, 22% in 2034); 30% through 2032. Battery storage added 2023 as standalone qualified (no solar pairing). Non-refundable but UNLIMITED carryforward (unused offsets future tax). Form 5695. Principal residence + 2nd home eligible; rentals NO. Includes labor + installation.
view.s25D.opt.basis_reduce: Basis reduction: 50% — applies to depreciation IF business use portion
view.s25D.opt.battery_alone: Standalone battery now qualifies — install without solar if grid backup desired
view.s25D.opt.battery_sizing: Battery 3+ kWh: avoid sub-3 kWh batteries (no credit)
view.s25D.opt.bundling: Bundle with state + utility rebates — § 25D applies to cost AFTER rebates (federal subtracts state)
view.s25D.opt.divided_ownership: Co-owners: each claims proportionate share based on % paid
view.s25D.opt.no_lease: Solar lease / PPA: NOT eligible — only OWNED systems qualify
view.s25D.opt.tax_planning: Coordinate with income year to use credit (Roth conv, capital gains realization)
view.s25E.elig.age: Model year at least 2 years older than current year
view.s25E.elig.battery_phev: PHEV: battery ≥ 7 kWh; BEV: no battery min stated; FCV: any
view.s25E.elig.dealer: Sold by IRS-registered dealer (NOT private party)
view.s25E.elig.first_resale: FIRST resale after original sale (not flippers / repeat sellers)
view.s25E.elig.income: MAGI: $75K / $112.5K / $150K (CURRENT or PRIOR yr — pick lower)
view.s25E.elig.no_domestic: NO domestic assembly / minerals / battery components requirement
view.s25E.elig.no_personal_use_before: Not used by purchasing taxpayer before
view.s25E.elig.price: Sale price ≤ $25,000 (includes options, dealer prep, document fees)
view.s25E.ex.ford_lightning: Used Ford Lightning (2022, $30K): NO CREDIT — over $25K cap
view.s25E.ex.mach_e: Used Mach-E (2022, $26K): NO CREDIT — over $25K cap
view.s25E.ex.prime: Used Prius Prime PHEV (2020, $18K): credit min($4,000, $5,400) = $4,000
view.s25E.ex.private_party: Private party sale (no dealer): NO CREDIT — must be qualified dealer
view.s25E.h1.title: // § 25E USED CLEAN VEHICLE
view.s25E.hint.intro: 30% of sale price OR $4,000 — LOWER applies. Sale price ≤ $25,000. Vehicle must be at least 2 model years older than current. MAGI limit: $75K single / $112.5K HoH / $150K MFJ. First resale only (not flippers). Qualified dealer required (registered with IRS Energy Credits Online). Buyer transfers credit to dealer at POS. One-per-three-year limit for buyer. Form 8936. NO domestic assembly / minerals / battery components requirements — unlike § 30D.
view.s25E.over_price_note: Sale price exceeds $25,000 cap. Negotiate price down or look at lower-priced inventory. For higher-priced new vehicles, consider § 30D (new) or § 45W (commercial / lease loophole) instead. § 25E is targeted at affordability for used EV market.
view.s25E.tx.dealer_3rd_party: 3rd-party finance ok; dealer must process credit transfer
view.s25E.tx.dealer_advance: Dealer receives advance payment from IRS (Form 8300)
view.s25E.tx.discount: Discount applied at POS — dealer registers via IRS Energy Credits Online
view.s25E.tx.no_clawback_safe_harbor: No clawback if buyer not aware of excess MAGI
view.s25E.tx.refundable_effect: Refundable effect: even if no tax liability, buyer gets discount
view.s25E.tx.repay_excess: Buyer repays on next return if MAGI exceeds limit
view.s25a.exp.529_no_double: 529 plan distributions: don't double-claim same dollars (electable allocation)
view.s25a.exp.books_aotc: AOTC ONLY: required books + supplies + equipment (whether or not from school)
view.s25a.exp.books_llc: LLC: required ONLY if purchased FROM school as condition of enrollment
view.s25a.exp.not_room: NOT room & board, transportation, insurance, medical, optional fees
view.s25a.exp.scholarship_offset: Reduce expenses by tax-free scholarships / grants / employer assistance
view.s25a.h1.title: // § 25A AOTC + LLC EDUCATION CREDITS
view.s25a.hint.intro: AOTC: $2,500/student/year — first 4 undergraduate years, ≥ half-time, no felony drug conviction. 40% refundable. LLC: $2,000 / RETURN (not per student) — 20% of first $10k. Unlimited years. Less generous but covers graduate / part-time. MAGI phase-outs: $80-90k single / $160-180k MFJ.
view.s25a.label.name: Student name
view.s25a.th.name: Name
view.s25c.eff.ac: Central AC: ENERGY STAR Most Efficient
view.s25c.eff.heat_pump: Heat pump: ENERGY STAR Most Efficient
view.s25c.eff.panel: Panel: ≥ 200 amp + supports clean energy upgrade
view.s25c.eff.products_qualified: Manufacturer must certify (PIN to be required by IRS 2025+)
view.s25c.eff.windows: Windows / skylights: ENERGY STAR Most Efficient + meet IECC 2021
view.s25c.h1.title: // § 25C ENERGY EFFICIENT HOME
view.s25c.hint.intro: IRA 2022 expanded: 30% of qualified expenses. Annual caps: $1,200 general + $2,000 heat pumps / heat pump water heaters / biomass stoves. Specific caps: $600 windows, $500 doors total ($250 each), $150 home energy audit, $600 panel. Through 12/31/2032. Existing home + primary residence required. PIN reporting required 2025+ via Form 8908.
view.s25c.rel.25d: § 25D Residential Clean Energy: 30% solar / battery / geothermal (no cap)
view.s25c.rel.30c: § 30C Alternative Fuel Refueling: 30% EV charger up to $1,000
view.s25c.rel.30d: § 30D Clean Vehicle Credit: up to $7,500 EVs
view.s25c.rel.heera: HEEHRA + HOMES Rebates (DOE, separate state-administered programs)
view.s25c.warning.eligibility: § 25C only applies to existing primary residences. New construction + secondary homes ineligible.
view.s263a.h1.title: // § 263A UNICAP + SMALL BIZ EXEMPTION
view.s263a.hint.intro: Producers + resellers must capitalize indirect costs into inventory and self-constructed assets — defers deduction until inventory sold or asset disposed. Small biz exemption: 3-yr avg gross receipts ≤ $30M (2024, indexed), no UNICAP required. TCJA raised threshold from $10M. Form 3115 method change required.
view.s263a.note.exempt_advice: Small biz exemption applies: expense indirect costs as incurred + simpler inventory accounting allowed (cash basis OR no inventory method per § 471(c)).
view.s263a.note.required: UNICAP required. Use Simplified Production Method (SPM) or Simplified Resale Method (SRM) absorption ratios. Form 3115 to change methods.
view.s263c.amt.65pct: Adjustment limited to 65% of net oil + gas income for the year
view.s263c.amt.formula: Preference = current IDC deduction - (current IDC if amortized over 10 yrs)
view.s263c.amt.individual_corp: Both individual + corp AMT (TCJA repealed corp AMT 2018-2022; now CAMT)
view.s263c.amt.integrated: Integrated oil co: still subject to AMT preference
view.s263c.amt.preference: AMT preference item: excess of IDC deduction over IDC-amortized basis
view.s263c.amt.regular_tax: Preference adds back to AMTI; not a tax — feeds into AMT calculation
view.s263c.amt.s56: § 56(g) related: passive activity loss disallowance for IDC
view.s263c.comp.dry_hole: Dry hole costs: §263(c) currently deductible (or capitalize)
view.s263c.comp.labor: Labor: drilling crew, supervision, geological labor
view.s263c.comp.supplies: Supplies: drilling mud, fluids, chemicals, abrasives
view.s263c.h1.title: // § 263(c) IDC ELECTION
view.s263c.hint.intro: Operators / working interest owners can ELECT to deduct Intangible Drilling Costs (IDC) CURRENTLY instead of capitalizing. IDCs: labor, fuel, repairs, hauling, supplies in drilling / development of wells. Independent producers + royalty owners: 100% deductible. Integrated oil cos: 70% deductible / 30% amortized 60 months. AMT preference: excess IDC over 65% of net oil + gas income. § 1254 recapture upon disposition. Reg § 1.612-4 election.
view.s263c.indep_note: Independent producer: 100% IDC current deduction + § 57(a)(2)(E) AMT exemption. Critical tax benefit for small / mid-size E&P companies. Limit: 75,000 bbl/day production. Combined with § 613A small producer depletion deduction (15%): substantial tax shelter for working interest investors.
view.s263c.rec.amount: Ordinary income up to amount of prior IDC + depletion deductions
view.s263c.rec.basis: Recapture basis: cumulative IDC + depletion above mineral basis
view.s263c.rec.disposition_types: Sale, exchange, abandonment, distribution all trigger
view.s263c.rec.holding_period_gain: Beyond recapture: § 1231 gain (capital) vs § 1245 if equipment recapture
view.s263c.rec.purpose: Recapture intangible costs deducted on disposition (parallel § 1245 / § 1250)
view.s263c.rec.related_party: Related party: § 1239 may recharacterize as ordinary income (if depreciable to buyer)
view.s263c.rec.s291_corp: § 291 corporate cutback: 20% of S corp / C corp § 1254 recapture (parallel)
view.s269.applies_note: § 269 likely applies: PRIMARY purpose tax avoidance + no business purpose. IRS may disallow NOL, credits, deductions in whole or part. Documents pre-acquisition: rebut with business purpose evidence (synergies, market expansion, talent acquisition). Pre-clearance: consider PLR before completing transaction.
view.s269.cons.disallow_nol: DISALLOW NOL carryover (in whole or part — discretion of Commissioner)
view.s269.cons.discretion: Commissioner has broad discretion to disallow some but not all
view.s269.cons.partial: Partial disallowance possible — depends on facts
view.s269.coord.s269_overlap: § 269 PRE-EMPTS even when § 382 also applies — broader anti-abuse
view.s269.coord.s356: § 356 (boot in reorg) coordinates with § 269 in tax-free reorgs
view.s269.coord.s367: § 367: outbound transfers anti-abuse for foreign acquisitions
view.s269.coord.s382: § 382: mechanical limit on NOL usage post-ownership change (annual ATE × LT rate)
view.s269.coord.s384: § 384: SRLY rule limits pre-acquisition losses against post-acquisition built-in gains
view.s269.coord.s482: § 482 transfer pricing: parallel anti-abuse for related-party transactions
view.s269.coord.s7701o: § 7701(o) Economic Substance: codified — meaningful change required + non-tax purpose
view.s269.coord.s7704: § 7704 PTP rules: prevent corporate shell using partnership structure
view.s269.h1.title: // § 269 ANTI-ABUSE ACQUISITION
view.s269.hint.intro: IRS DISALLOWS deductions, credits, allowances from acquired corp if PRIMARY purpose = tax avoidance. § 269(a)(1): person acquires CONTROL (50%) of corp. § 269(a)(2): corp acquires PROPERTY w/ carryover basis. Burden: IRS (initially), then taxpayer to rebut. Overlap: § 382 NOL limit, § 384 SRLY, § 7701(o) economic substance. § 269 PRE-EMPTS even when § 382 also limits — covers SRLY-style and entity-level transactions broader than § 382 ownership change.
view.s269.test.bargain_purchase: Bargain purchase below FMV due to deferred tax assets = indicia of tax motive
view.s269.test.business_purpose: Business purpose can SAVE acquisition even if tax savings significant
view.s269.test.factual: Fact-intensive: written documents, internal memoranda, board minutes scrutinized
view.s269.test.large_nol: Large NOL with no operations = clear § 269 territory
view.s269.test.shell_corps: Shell corps + dormant targets = high § 269 risk
view.s269.test.tax_motive: Tax motive must be MORE THAN 50% of purpose (primary)
view.s269a.h1.title: // § 269A PERSONAL SERVICE CORPORATION
view.s269a.hint.intro: IRS may reallocate income, deductions, credits between PSC + owner-employees if substantially all services to one entity AND principal purpose tax avoidance.
view.s274.empty: No entries logged for this year.
view.s274.h1.title: // § 274 MEALS / GIFTS / ENTERTAINMENT
view.s274.hint.intro: Post-TCJA (2018+): business entertainment 100% DISALLOWED (sports tickets, golf, theater, concerts). Business meals 50% deductible (briefly 100% restaurants 2021-22 under CAA). Client gifts capped at $25/recipient/year (de minimis branded items < $4 with logo exempt).
view.s274.rule.de_minimis: De minimis branded items (< $4, logo) exempt from $25 cap
view.s274.rule.gift_25: Client gift: $25/recipient/yr (per donor not per gift)
view.s274.rule.meal_50: Business meal with client / prospect: 50% deductible
view.s274.rule.travel_meal: Travel meal (away from tax home): 50% deductible
view.s280c.h1.title: // § 280C CREDITS DEDUCTION DISALLOWANCE
view.s280c.hint.intro: § 280C(a) wage credits reduce § 162 deduction. § 280C(c) R&D credit: reduce § 174 basis OR elect reduced 79% credit. Post-2022 § 174 capitalization changes the calculus.
view.s280e.case.alpenglow: Alpenglow Botanicals v. United States (10th Cir. 2018) — § 280E constitutionality upheld
view.s280e.case.alterman: Alterman v. Comm'r (2018) — denied § 471(c) attempt to recharacterize as inventory
view.s280e.case.champ: Champ v. Comm'r (2007) — separate caregiving activity escapes § 280E
view.s280e.case.harborside: Harborside Health Center v. Comm'r (2018) — COGS strictly limited to § 471 production
view.s280e.case.olive: Olive v. Comm'r (9th Cir. 2015) — caregiving DOES NOT escape if too entwined
view.s280e.cogs.indirect_production: Indirect production: utilities, depreciation, supervisor wages allocable to grow rooms
view.s280e.dis.rent: Dispensary rent (production area allocable to COGS)
view.s280e.h1.title: // § 280E CANNABIS DISALLOWANCE
view.s280e.hint.intro: Schedule I / II trafficker businesses CANNOT deduct ordinary business expenses. Only COGS via § 471 escapes — direct + indirect production / acquisition costs. Result: effective tax rates can EXCEED 70%. Champ v. Comm'r (2007) carve-out: separate non-cannabis activity (caregiving services) keeps its own deductions. Olive v. Comm'r (9th Cir. 2015) tightened that exception.
view.s280e.note.future: Federal rescheduling (DEA Schedule I → III proposal pending) would END § 280E disallowance. Strong push from MORE Act and SAFE Banking Act.
view.s280f.h1.title: // § 280F LUXURY AUTO DEPRECIATION
view.s280f.heavy.ev: Tesla Model X (most builds), Rivian R1T/R1S, F-150 Lightning
view.s280f.heavy.luxury: Range Rover, Mercedes G-Wagen, Lexus LX, BMW X7, Audi Q7/Q8
view.s280f.heavy.note: Always verify the SPECIFIC model + trim's GVWR on the manufacturer doorjamb sticker
view.s280f.heavy.suv: Cadillac Escalade, Chevy Suburban / Tahoe, Ford Expedition, GMC Yukon
view.s280f.heavy.trucks: F-150 / F-250 / F-350, Silverado 1500+, Ram 1500+, Tundra
view.s280f.hint.intro: Caps depreciation on passenger autos + light trucks. Heavy SUVs (GVWR > 6,000 lbs) escape § 280F, get up to $30,500 § 179 + bonus. Personal use must be ≤ 50% to take bonus/§179. 2024 first-year limits: $20,400 with bonus, $12,400 without. Year 2: $19,800. Year 3: $11,900. Year 4+: $7,160/yr.
view.s280f.recapture.body: If business use drops to ≤ 50% in any later year, § 280F(d)(1) requires recapture of all bonus + § 179 deducted in prior years — included as ordinary income. Keep contemporaneous mileage log (Form 4562 line 30).
view.s280f.warning.under50: Business use ≤ 50% — bonus depreciation + § 179 not available. Straight-line over 5 yrs only.
view.s280g.clean.75pct: Vote ≥ 75% of OUTSTANDING shares (not just present)
view.s280g.clean.adequate: Adequate disclosure of all material facts to shareholders
view.s280g.clean.exclusions: Disqualified persons + their relatives + control entities EXCLUDED from vote
view.s280g.clean.smb: Must qualify as Small Business Corp (no public stock)
view.s280g.clean.tax_savings: If cleansed: no 20% excise + corp gets full deduction
view.s280g.clean.timing: Vote BEFORE payment + binding right to refuse
view.s280g.h1.title: // § 280G GOLDEN PARACHUTE EXCISE
view.s280g.hint.intro: Change-of-control comp ≥ 3× base amount (5-yr avg Box 1 W-2): entire amount over 1× base is 'excess parachute payment'. 20% excise on recipient + DISALLOWED deduction to corp on excess. Applies to 'disqualified individuals' — officers, > 1% shareholders, highly compensated (top 1%). Private cos: § 280G(b)(5) cleansing by 75%+ shareholder vote.
view.s280g.work.best_after_tax: 'Best-after-tax' cutback clause: cap at lower of 2.99× or threshold that maximizes net
view.s280g.work.cleansing_vote: Private co: shareholder cleansing vote (75% threshold)
view.s280g.work.gross_up: Cap on parachute at 2.99× base (avoid 3× threshold)
view.s280g.work.gross_up_pay: Gross-up payment: corp pays excise tax — but gross-up itself is parachute
view.s280g.work.reasonable: Allocate to reasonable post-acquisition comp / non-compete (not parachute)
view.s280g.work.severance_unwind: Restructure severance to spread over post-CoC employment
view.s280g.work.vested: Pre-existing vested benefits = not parachute (must predate change of control)
view.s302.attr.cant_waive_entity: Cannot waive entity attribution (corp, partnership, trust)
view.s302.attr.corporation: Corporation: 50%+ shareholder treated as owning corp's stock pro-rata
view.s302.attr.family: Family: spouse + children + grandchildren + parents (NOT siblings)
view.s302.attr.options: Options: option to acquire treated as owned
view.s302.attr.trust: Trust + estate: full attribution to beneficiaries by interest
view.s302.attr.waiver: Family attribution may be waived with 10-yr look-back agreement (§ 302(c))
view.s302.div.basis_lost: Basis NOT recovered against dividend — added to remaining shares' basis
view.s302.div.foreign: Foreign shareholder: 30% FDAP withholding (or treaty rate)
view.s302.div.no_capital: No long-term capital gains rate, no loss recognition
view.s302.div.ordinary: Full amount taxed as DIVIDEND to extent of E&P (no basis recovery)
view.s302.div.qualified: May be qualified dividend if individual + 60-day holding + domestic
view.s302.dividend_note: Dividend treatment: basis is NOT recovered against dividend amount. Instead added to remaining shares' basis (or lost if complete termination via constructive). This is the major punishment of dividend characterization.
view.s302.h1.title: // § 302 STOCK REDEMPTION
view.s302.hint.intro: Redemption = corporation buys back its own stock from shareholder. Sale treatment (gain / loss) OR § 301 dividend (ordinary income to extent of E&P). Sale tests § 302(b): (1) not essentially equivalent, (2) substantially disproportionate (post < 80% of pre + post < 50% total), (3) complete termination, (4) partial liquidation. § 318 attribution — family + entity + options. Waiver of family attribution with 10-year look-back agreement.
view.s303.h1.title: // § 303 ESTATE TAX REDEMPTION
view.s303.hint.intro: Closely-held stock redemption tax-free up to estate tax + funeral + admin expenses. Requires stock > 35% adjusted gross estate. Pairs with § 6166 installment.
view.s304.h1.title: // § 304 RELATED CORPORATION REDEMPTION
view.s304.hint.intro: Anti-abuse: sale of stock to related corp treated as redemption + § 301 distribution. § 304(a)(1) brother-sister + § 304(a)(2) parent-sub.
view.s305.b1: Distribution in lieu of money: shareholder option of cash OR stock
view.s305.b2: Disproportionate distribution: some shareholders receive cash, others stock — increased proportionate interest
view.s305.b3: Common AND preferred distributions: common to some shareholders + preferred to others
view.s305.b4: Preferred stock on preferred: distribution of preferred on preferred (except antidilution adj)
view.s305.b5: Convertible preferred: where any shareholder will have increased proportionate interest
view.s305.basis.example: Example: $10K basis on 100 shares; 1-for-1 stock split; $5K basis on each lot
view.s305.basis.holding: Holding period of new stock TACKS to old stock
view.s305.basis.preferred_common: Preferred + common: allocate by FMV ratio (preferred at par or market)
view.s305.basis.recordkeeping: Form 8937 + 1099-DIV box reporting for issuer + broker
view.s305.basis.relative: Allocate basis between old + new stock by RELATIVE FMV on distribution date
view.s305.basis.s306: § 306 'tainted stock': bailout-prevention rule on later sale of preferred
view.s305.basis.short_term: Cash in lieu of fractional shares: reported on 1099-B as sale; partial gain
view.s305.basis.tax_dividend: If § 305(b) makes it taxable: basis = FMV at distribution (full step-up)
view.s305.c.amount: Amount: FMV of increased interest (use binomial / Black-Scholes for convertibles)
view.s305.c.anti_dilution: Anti-dilution adjustments: safe harbor if proportionate to ALL stock
view.s305.c.arrearages: Accrued / suspended dividend arrearages on preferred: treated as deemed dividends in some cases
view.s305.c.conversion: Conversion ratio adjustment: increases conversion advantage of one class vs others
view.s305.c.pik_preferred: PIK preferred: accrual of dividends paid in additional preferred = deemed dist
view.s305.c.reporting: Form 1099-DIV reporting + Form 8937 corporate action issuer reporting
view.s305.c.timing: Timing: economic accrual — not just legal payment date
view.s305.c.warrants: Warrants + options: § 305(d) treats holders as shareholders for these rules
view.s305.c_note: § 305(c) deemed dividend triggered. PIK preferred accruals + conversion ratio adjustments cause economic-substance taxation even without legal payment. Apply Form 8937 reporting + 1099-DIV broker-level adjustment.
view.s305.h1.title: // § 305 STOCK DIVIDENDS
view.s305.hint.intro: § 305(a): Stock dividend = TAX-FREE; just splits ownership pro-rata. Holding period tacks; basis allocated by relative FMV. § 305(b) EXCEPTIONS (5 categories) trigger ORDINARY DIVIDEND treatment: (1) cash or stock option, (2) disproportionate distribution, (3) common + preferred to different shareholders, (4) preferred-stock-on-preferred, (5) convertible preferred. § 305(c): DEEMED dividends — conversion ratio adjustments, accrued PIK on preferred, arrearages.
view.s30C.elig.50pct_residential: Residential property: 50%+ used for personal — separate $1K limit applies
view.s30C.elig.address: Address-based: enter installation address to verify eligibility
view.s30C.elig.coordinates: Mapper uses geographic coordinates from US Census 2020 + BLS data
view.s30C.elig.either: EITHER non-urban OR low-income qualifies (not both required)
view.s30C.elig.lic: Low-income community: § 45D(e) Census tract — poverty rate ≥ 20% OR income ≤ 80% statewide
view.s30C.elig.non_urban: Non-urban: NOT in Census Bureau urban area (cities + suburbs)
view.s30C.elig.previously_eligible: Pre-2023: ALL locations eligible — IRA 2022 added geographic restriction
view.s30C.h1.title: // § 30C ALT. FUEL CHARGER
view.s30C.hint.intro: EV charger + alternative fuel infrastructure credit. Business: 30% (with wage/apprenticeship) up to $100K per item. Personal: 30% / $1,000 cap. IRA 2022 restriction: MUST be located in non-urban area OR low-income community census tract (8911 mapper). Construction begin before 1/1/2033; placed in service after 12/31/2022. § 6418 transferability for business; 20-yr carryforward. Form 8911.
view.s30C.loc_note: Location FAIL: IRA 2022 restricts § 30C to non-urban areas + low-income communities only. Pre-2023 installations may qualify under older rules. Verify via 8911 mapper at irs.gov. Address-based lookup uses Census 2020 boundaries — borderline addresses may be eligible.
view.s30C.prop.bidirectional: V2G bidirectional charging: eligible if meets criteria
view.s30C.prop.cost_basis: Includes: equipment + installation labor + permits + ancillary costs
view.s30C.prop.ev_charger: EV chargers: Level 2 (residential), DC fast charging (commercial)
view.s30D.elig.assembly: Final assembly: must occur in North America (FuelEconomy.gov verifies)
view.s30D.elig.battery_kwh: Battery capacity: 7 kWh min for plug-in hybrid (PHEV); BEV no min
view.s30D.elig.foreign_entity: FEOC (Foreign Entity of Concern) restriction: starting 2024 batteries, 2025 critical minerals
view.s30D.elig.gross_vehicle: Gross Vehicle Weight Rating: ≤ 14,000 lbs (commercial trucks use § 45W)
view.s30D.elig.magi: MAGI limit: $300K MFJ / $225K HoH / $150K single (CURRENT or PRIOR yr — pick lower)
view.s30D.fail_note: Not eligible. Common failures: MSRP exceeds cap, MAGI exceeds limit, vehicle assembled outside NA, missing critical minerals / battery components qualifications. Consider used EV (§ 25E) or commercial route (§ 45W) — different rules + no income / MSRP limit.
view.s30D.h1.title: // § 30D CLEAN VEHICLE
view.s30D.hint.intro: Up to $7,500: $3,750 critical minerals + $3,750 battery components (IRA 2022). Income limit: $300K MFJ / $225K HoH / $150K single (MAGI, current OR prior year). MSRP cap: $80K SUVs/vans/trucks; $55K cars. Final assembly: MUST occur in NORTH AMERICA. Transfer at dealer (2024+): get $7,500 off price at purchase. Form 8936. Used EV: § 25E ≤ $4,000 / 30% (separate, lower limits).
view.s30D.tx.cash: Dealer receives advance payment from IRS (Form 8300)
view.s30D.tx.dealer: Buyer transfers credit to dealer at point of sale → discount $ off MSRP
view.s30D.tx.dealer_must_register: Dealer registers via IRS Energy Credits Online (ECO) portal
view.s30D.tx.income_excess: If MAGI exceeds limit retroactively: REPAY credit on next return
view.s30D.tx.lease_loophole: § 45W lease 'loophole' (commercial credit) — dealer credit available even if vehicle non-qualifying for § 30D
view.s30D.tx.no_tax_owed: Buyer benefits even if no tax liability — non-refundable becomes refundable in practice
view.s30D.tx.repayment_safe_harbor: No clawback if buyer not aware of income excess
view.s30D.tx.report_form_15400: Report on Form 8936 + 8936 Schedule A; dealer issues Time-of-Sale Report
view.s311.corp.character: Character of gain: same as if sold (ordinary, capital, depreciation recapture)
view.s311.corp.ep_reduction: E&P reduced by greater of FMV or liab (§ 312(b))
view.s311.corp.gain: § 311(b): GAIN recognized on appreciated property (deemed sale at FMV)
view.s311.corp.liab: If liab > FMV, treated as FMV = liab (Crane / § 7701(g))
view.s311.corp.no_loss: § 311(a) general rule: NO gain / loss recognized
view.s311.corp.no_loss_b: § 311(b)(1): NO LOSS allowed on depreciated property — asymmetric
view.s311.corp.s355_distinction: § 355 spinoffs: different rules (§ 361 — corp side, § 355 — shareholder)
view.s311.h1.title: // § 311 CORP DISTRIBUTIONS
view.s311.hint.intro: § 311(a): Distributing corp generally recognizes NO gain / loss on non-liquidating distribution. § 311(b): EXCEPT corp recognizes GAIN on APPRECIATED property (FMV > basis). NO LOSS on depreciated property — asymmetric. Liabilities assumed: treated as cash; FMV = greater of FMV or liab. Shareholder side: § 301 dividend (ordinary) to extent of E&P, then return of capital, then capital gain. Liquidating: § 336 (general gain) or § 337 (≥ 80% sub).
view.s311.no_loss_note: Asymmetric § 311(b): corp gain on appreciation recognized BUT corp loss on depreciation NEVER recognized. Distribute LOSS property to reduce E&P without tax cost; sell GAIN property to control timing.
view.s311.plan.basis_step: Basis step-up to shareholder may offset corp gain economically
view.s311.plan.dist_loss_assets: Distribute LOSS property first (no loss recognized but reduce E&P / basis)
view.s311.plan.installment: § 453 installment method on sale → defer cash + gain
view.s311.plan.related_party: § 267 related party rules: loss disallowed even on outright sale
view.s311.plan.s_corp: S-corp pass-through: gain flows to shareholders (single tax) — better than C-corp
view.s311.plan.sell_gain_keep: Better: SELL gain assets first (recognize gain) → distribute cash
view.s311.share.basis: Shareholder takes FMV BASIS in distributed property (§ 301(d))
view.s311.share.corp_drd: Corp shareholder: § 243 DRD (50/65/100%) may reduce dividend tax
view.s311.share.dividend: Dividend (ordinary) to extent of E&P (current + accumulated)
view.s311.share.fmv: Amount received = FMV of distributed property + liab discharged
view.s311.share.gain: Remaining = capital gain (LTCG if stock held > 1 yr)
view.s311.share.holding_period: Holding period for distributed property starts on distribution date
view.s311.share.qualified_div: Qualified dividend rate (20%) if individual + 60-day holding + domestic
view.s311.share.return_capital: Return of capital — reduces stock basis to zero
view.s318.h1.title: // § 318 CONSTRUCTIVE OWNERSHIP
view.s318.hint.intro: 5 attribution rules across family + entities + options. Used by § 302/§ 304 redemptions + § 1361 S-corp count + § 4975 prohibited transactions.
view.s32.h1.title: // § 32 EARNED INCOME TAX CREDIT
view.s32.hint.intro: Refundable credit for low-income workers. 2024 max $7,830 (3+ kids). § 32(d) $11,600 investment income cliff. SSN required.
view.s332.h1.title: // § 332 SUB LIQUIDATION (TAX-FREE)
view.s332.hint.intro: § 332: Parent corp recognizes NO gain / loss on liquidation of ≥ 80%-owned subsidiary. § 337: Sub recognizes NO gain / loss on distribution to ≥ 80% parent. § 334(b): Parent inherits SUB's INSIDE BASIS in assets — NO step-up. § 381: Parent inherits NOL, E&P, credits, methods. Requires PLAN of liquidation + complete within ONE TAX YEAR (or 3 years per IRS approval). Minority shareholders: § 331 normal taxable. Insolvent sub: § 332 does NOT apply (parent recognizes loss under § 165).
view.s332.insol.no_332: § 332 NOT available if sub insolvent (no property distributed for stock)
view.s332.insol.no_337: Sub still recognizes gain / loss on asset distribution (§ 337 not avail)
view.s332.insol.no_381: NO § 381 carryover — sub attributes lost (NOL, E&P, credits)
view.s332.insol.s165: Parent recognizes worthless stock loss under § 165(g)(1) — ordinary if affiliated
view.s332.insol.timing: Worthlessness identified — usually formal dissolution or bankruptcy
view.s332.insol.tradeoff: Tradeoff: parent gets ordinary loss BUT sub's attributes lost forever
view.s332.label.outside: Parent outside basis in sub stock ($)
view.s332.minority_note: Minority shareholders (< 20%) DO NOT get § 332 — they recognize gain / loss under § 331 (sale or exchange treatment). Plan for separate tax computation at minority level.
view.s332.req.cessation: Sub ceases to exist for tax purposes (often state-law dissolution)
view.s332.req.foreign_sub: Foreign sub: § 367(b) regulations apply — may trigger inclusion of all E&P
view.s332.req.full_payment: Property distributed in complete cancellation of stock
view.s332.req.minority_taxable: Minority shareholders (< 20%) get § 331 fully taxable treatment
view.s332.req.ownership: Parent corp owns ≥ 80% of sub stock by vote AND value (§ 1504(a)(2))
view.s332.req.plan: Plan of liquidation adopted (formal corporate action)
view.s332.req.timing: Complete within 1 tax year of plan, OR up to 3 years w/ approval § 332(b)(3)
view.s332.s381.exception: SRLY rules limit pre-acq NOL use against post-acq income (cons. ret. only)
view.s332.s381.nol: NOL: parent inherits, subject to § 382 limitation if ownership change in sub
view.s336.disqualified_note: Loss DISALLOWED — § 336(d)(1) disqualified property + related-party distribution. Allocate loss-property to UNRELATED shareholders, OR sell BEFORE liquidation, OR avoid § 351 contributions within 2 yrs of planned liquidation.
view.s336.h1.title: // § 336 CORP LIQUIDATION
view.s336.hint.intro: § 336(a): Corp recognizes GAIN AND LOSS on liquidating distribution at FMV. Contrast § 311 (non-liquidating, gain ONLY). § 336(d)(1): NO LOSS on distribution of 'disqualified property' (contributed in § 351 / capital contrib within 2 yrs to related party). § 336(d)(2): 'Tax avoidance' loss reduced if basis > FMV. Shareholder side: § 331 — gain / loss on stock; FMV basis in distributed property. § 332 alternate: tax-free if 80%+ parent (§ 337 for sub).
view.s336.loss.allocation: Loss allocated pro-rata across distributee shareholders
view.s336.loss.disqual_period: 5-year period: contribution + tax avoidance presumed within 2 yrs
view.s336.loss.disqualified: 'Disqualified property' = property contributed in § 351 / capital contrib + tax-avoidance purpose
view.s336.loss.related: Related party: § 267 — > 50% owner, family, controlled corps
view.s336.loss.s336d1: § 336(d)(1): NO LOSS on distribution of disqualified property to related party (§ 267)
view.s336.loss.s336d2: § 336(d)(2): 'tax avoidance' loss reduced if asset's basis > FMV at contribution date
view.s336.loss.s336d3: § 336(d)(3): no loss to 80% parent under § 332 (general non-recognition)
view.s336.s336e.basis_step: Buyer gets stepped-up basis to AGUB (similar to 338(h)(10))
view.s336.s336e.consent: Seller-side election; no buyer consent required
view.s336.s336e.election: Election: attached to seller's tax return for year of sale
view.s336.s336e.gain_seller: Gain recognized at seller / consolidated group level
view.s336.s336e.s_corp: S-corp target OR consolidated sub eligible (similar to § 338(h)(10))
view.s336.s336e.s_corp_basis: S-corp shareholders adjust basis for inside-built gain
view.s336.s336e.scope: Stock sale to UNRELATED party by single SELLER (not buyer) recharacterized as asset sale
view.s336.s336e.use_case: Useful when buyer is reluctant to join § 338(h)(10) but seller wants asset-sale treatment
view.s336.share.amount: Amount realized = FMV of property + cash + liability discharged (not assumed)
view.s336.share.depreciation_recap: No depreciation recapture passes through to shareholder (corp recognizes at § 336)
view.s336.share.gain_loss: Gain / loss = amount realized − stock basis (capital character)
view.s336.share.holding: Holding period: from original acquisition; LTCG if > 1 yr
view.s336.share.installment: § 453(h): installment note received in liquidation reported on installment method
view.s336.share.minority: Minority shareholders (< 20%): always § 331 (even if 80%+ parent uses § 332)
view.s336.share.s453B_d: § 453B(d): NO recognition on dist of installment notes (deferred)
view.s338.cls.actively_traded: Class II: actively traded securities + CDs
view.s338.cls.cash: Class I: cash + bank deposits (no allocation)
view.s338.cls.goodwill: Class VII: goodwill + going concern value
view.s338.cls.intangible_non_goodwill: Class VI: intangibles not in Class VII (§ 197, customer lists)
view.s338.cls.receivables: Class III: receivables, mortgages, credit card
view.s338.cls.residual: Allocation: by FMV within each class, residual to Class VI/VII (goodwill)
view.s338.election_note: Election produces positive NPV. Coordinate § 1060 / § 338 residual allocation method to maximize Class VI/VII (15-yr § 197 amortization) over Class IV/V (longer depreciation).
view.s338.h1.title: // § 338 STOCK→ASSET ELECTION
view.s338.hint.intro: Stock purchase treated as ASSET PURCHASE for tax. § 338(g): unilateral by buyer (Buyer pays target's immediate gain tax with no offset to Seller). § 338(h)(10): joint election (Buyer + Seller) for S-corp OR consolidated-group target — gain recognized at SELLER level (one tax). Target gets step-up to AGUB (Aggregated Grossed-Up Basis). Tradeoff: immediate gain tax at acquisition vs step-up (depreciation / amortization) recovery. Filed on Form 8023.
view.s338.loss.consolidated: LOSE: Acquirer can't use sub's losses against group (SRLY) anyway
view.s338.loss.high_appreciation: LOSE: Target has high appreciated assets (huge gain tax)
view.s338.loss.nol_destruction: LOSE: Target has NOLs that would otherwise carry over (lost in election)
view.s338.loss.qsst_disabled: LOSE: S-corp ESBT / QSST trust beneficiary may disqualify
view.s338.no_election_note: Election cost exceeds step-up benefit. Likely better off WITHOUT § 338 election — accept stock-purchase treatment, retain target's NOLs / E&P, no immediate gain tax.
view.s338.win.foreign_338g: § 338(g) wins for foreign target (gain often deferred / minimal foreign-tax burden)
view.s338.win.high_basis: WIN: Target has high inside basis already (small step-up cost)
view.s338.win.high_intangibles: WIN: Target has significant intangibles → § 197 15-year amortization
view.s338.win.s_corp: § 338(h)(10) win for S-corp targets — single level gain
view.s351.boot.basis: Transferor basis in entity = property basis + gain - boot - liabilities
view.s351.boot.cash: Cash + non-stock property received = BOOT, gain recognized to extent of boot
view.s351.boot.holding: Holding period of stock / interest TACKS
view.s351.boot.liabilities: Liabilities assumed = BOOT for § 357(c) gain if liabilities > basis (corp only)
view.s351.boot.partnership_liability: Partnership liabilities: increase / decrease partner's basis under § 752
view.s351.h1.title: // § 351 / § 721 TAX-FREE FORMATION
view.s351.hint.intro: Contribute property to corp / partnership for ownership interest — generally no gain recognized. § 351: contributor + co-contributors collectively own ≥ 80% post-transfer. § 721: any contribution to partnership tax-free (no control test). § 357 boot trap: liabilities assumed by entity in excess of contributor's basis triggers GAIN. § 1032: entity recognizes no gain on issuance of its own stock for property.
view.s351.note.disregarded: Single-member LLC = disregarded entity — no taxable event, owner directly holds property for tax purposes.
view.s355.case.ebay_paypal: eBay → PayPal (2015): clean Type D divisive
view.s355.case.morris_trust_origin: Morris Trust v. Commissioner (1966): merger-spin combo upheld → § 355(e) enacted 1997
view.s355.case.no_ruling: 'No-rule' areas: 2003 Rev. Proc. 2003-48 reduced rulings significantly
view.s355.case.private_letter: PLR practice: 6-9 months pre-spin work; ruling cost $40-50K
view.s355.h1.title: // § 355 SPIN-OFF / SPLIT-OFF
view.s355.hint.intro: Tax-free distribution of CONTROLLED CORP STOCK. Requirements: (1) 5-yr active business EACH side, (2) control (80%) distributed, (3) not a device for dividend, (4) business purpose, (5) continuity of interest (50%+ owners). Spin-off: pro-rata (shareholders keep both). Split-off: surrender Distributing stock. Split-up: Distributing liquidates → shareholders get Sub1 + Sub2. Anti-abuse: § 355(d) 50% acquisition triggers gain. § 355(e) Morris Trust.
view.s355.morris_note: § 355(e) Morris Trust triggered: 50%+ acquisition of either D or C within 2-yr window (before / after) causes Distributing to recognize gain (not loss). Plan against post-spin acquisitions for at least 24 months to preserve tax-free status.
view.s355.req.active: Active Trade or Business 5-year rule: § 355(b) — BOTH Distributing + Controlled engaged for 5 years
view.s355.req.cobe: Continuity of Business Enterprise: BOTH sides continue historic business
view.s355.req.continuity: Continuity of Interest: 50%+ Distributing shareholders retain stock in spun-off
view.s355.req.control: Distribute Control: 80% vote AND 80% value of Controlled stock
view.s355.req.device: Not Used Principally as Device for distribution of E&P (§ 355(a)(1)(B))
view.s355.req.no_355e: No § 355(e) 'Morris Trust' 50% acquisition of either D or C within 2-yr window
view.s357.a.basis_reduce: Transferor's stock basis REDUCED by liabilities assumed
view.s357.a.bookkeeping: No current tax, but built-in gain preserved
view.s357.a.example: Example: transfer property basis $100K, FMV $300K, liab $80K → no gain; stock basis = $20K
view.s357.a.no_boot: Assumption of liabilities NOT treated as boot
view.s357.a.rationale: Economic substance: transferor's net economic position unchanged
view.s357.b.all_boot: ENTIRE liability amount treated as boot → gain to extent of FMV − basis ratio
view.s357.b.cash_out: Transferor extracts cash via debt → § 357(b) more likely applies
view.s357.b.intent: IRS often finds avoidance when liability incurred shortly before transfer
view.s357.b.recent_debt: Recent debt + cash extraction = 'tax avoidance' presumption
view.s357.b.refinance: Refinancing before incorporation: scrutinized as bootstrap
view.s357.b.trigger: Tax avoidance motive OR no business purpose for transfer of liability
view.s357.c.basic: Liabilities ASSUMED > TOTAL BASIS of property transferred → recognize gain
view.s357.c.basis_floor: Transferor's stock basis cannot go below zero — § 357(c) prevents negative basis
view.s357.c.character: Character: same as if property sold (capital, ordinary, recapture)
view.s357.c.s357c_3: § 357(c)(3): liabilities arising from deductible-when-paid items NOT counted (accounts payable)
view.s357.c.s361_reorg: § 361 reorg context: § 357(c) does NOT apply to plain D reorgs
view.s357.c.solution_boot: Workaround: take back NOTE / boot for excess (recognize gain anyway but more flexibility)
view.s357.h1.title: // § 357 LIABILITIES IN § 351
view.s357.hint.intro: § 357(a): Liability assumption generally NOT boot (no immediate gain). § 357(b): Tax avoidance / no business purpose → ENTIRE liability treated as boot → gain to transferor. § 357(c): Liabilities EXCEED basis → gain to extent of excess. Basis impact: transferor's stock basis = property basis − liabilities; transferee corporation inherits transferor's basis in property. Coordinate with § 351 control test.
view.s357.label.other_basis: Transferor's basis in OTHER prop transferred ($)
view.s357.s357c_note: § 357(c) gain triggered — liabilities exceeded basis. Workarounds: (1) contribute additional high-basis property to absorb excess, (2) transferor retains liability instead of letting corp assume it, (3) take back installment note to spread gain.
view.s362.h1.title: // § 362 CORPORATE BASIS ON TAX-FREE TRANSFER
view.s362.hint.intro: § 362(a) carryover + § 362(b) reorg + § 362(e)(2) NBIL anti-loss reduction OR election to reduce outside basis. § 1223(2) holding period tacking.
view.s367a.basic.alternative: Alternative: § 367(d) IP / § 482 transfer pricing for ongoing royalties
view.s367a.basic.character: Character of gain: same as if sold at FMV (ordinary, capital, recapture)
view.s367a.basic.holding_period: Holding period: as if disposed of (resets in transferee's hands)
view.s367a.basic.no_corp: Foreign transferee TREATED AS NOT A CORPORATION
view.s367a.basic.policy: Policy: prevent erosion of US tax base on built-in gain
view.s367a.exc.active_definition: Active TIB: § 1.367(a)-2 substantial managerial / operational activities
view.s367a.exc.active_pre_tcja: PRE-TCJA: active business exception EXEMPT property used in active foreign trade or business
view.s367a.exc.foreign_use: Foreign use: must be USED predominantly OUTSIDE US
view.s367a.exc.never_qualify: NEVER qualify: inventory, depreciable US-use property, installment notes, receivables, foreign currency obligations
view.s367a.exc.recapture: Recapture upon disposition within 5 yrs (Gain Recognition Agreement)
view.s367a.exc.remaining: REMAINING (post-TCJA): tangible property used in ACTIVE TRADE / BUSINESS outside US
view.s367a.exc.tcja_narrowed: TCJA 2017: REMOVED active business exception for IP, foreign goodwill, going concern
view.s367a.exc.transferee_country: Transferee country: subject to subF, GILTI rules + ECI overlay
view.s367a.gra.6038b: Coordinate with § 6038B reporting (Form 926 — Return for U.S. Transferors of Property)
view.s367a.gra.failure: Failure: full gain recognition retroactive + interest + penalties
view.s367a.gra.formal: Formal written agreement + IRS specific procedure
view.s367a.gra.lock_in: 5-year period locked in — cannot revoke unless replaced by new GRA
view.s367a.gra.purpose: 5-year deferral if transferor enters GRA + commits to recognize gain on triggering events
view.s367a.gra.s_corp: S-corp transferor: shareholders bear gain consequences
view.s367a.gra.triggering: Triggering events: sale of stock, dispositions of property, change in basis
view.s367a.h1.title: // § 367(a) OUTBOUND TANGIBLE
view.s367a.hint.intro: US transferor recognizes GAIN on outbound transfer of property to foreign corp. § 367(a)(1) general rule: foreign transferee treated as NOT a corp → no § 351 deferral. § 367(a)(3) ACTIVE business exception (HISTORICAL, TCJA 2017 NARROWED scope — for ACTIVE TRADE / BUSINESS US TANGIBLE property). Inventory + depreciable + installment obligations + receivables: NEVER qualify for exception. Compare § 367(d) IP outbound (annual deemed royalty). § 6038B reporting required.
view.s367a.no_filing_note: § 6038B Form 926 REQUIRED for outbound transfers. Failure to file: $100,000 minimum penalty (or 10% of FMV if higher, capped at $100K). Statute of limitations remains open for 3 yrs after late filing. Penalty waived only for reasonable cause + GOOD FAITH effort.
view.s367d.comp.amortization: Annual inclusion declines as IP's productive capacity decreases
view.s367d.comp.character: Character: ORDINARY royalty income (not capital gain)
view.s367d.comp.no_capital_gain: No capital gain even on appreciated IP at transfer (annual royalty instead)
view.s367d.comp.no_loss: No loss on transfer even if FMV < basis
view.s367d.comp.royalty: Annual royalty: arm's-length comparable (CUT, Profit Split, Income Method)
view.s367d.comp.subF_overlap: Subject to subF / GILTI if held in CFC: avoid double-tax via § 78 gross-up
view.s367d.comp.transferee_basis: Transferee basis = FMV at transfer (capital expenditure)
view.s367d.comp.useful_life: Useful life: estimated economic life of IP (typically 5-15 years)
view.s367d.coord.s250: § 250 FDII: incentive to keep IP in US (13.125% effective rate)
view.s367d.coord.s367a: § 367(a)(1): tangible property outbound — gain recognition (vs § 367(d) ongoing royalty)
view.s367d.coord.s367a3: § 367(a)(3): active trade/business exception (TCJA removed for IP)
view.s367d.coord.s367b: § 367(b) inbound regulations — exit-charge for CFC liquidations
view.s367d.coord.s482: § 482 transfer pricing: same arm's-length standard applies on royalty determination
view.s367d.coord.s936: § 936 possessions tax credit — repealed but historical context
view.s367d.coord.s951a: § 951A GILTI: IP income now subject to GILTI inclusion if held in CFC
view.s367d.coord.s956: § 956 investment in US property: keeps IP from US shareholder access
view.s367d.csa.amazon_altera: Tax Court precedent: Amazon (CSA cost methodology), Altera (SBC must be shared)
view.s367d.csa.entry_fee: Foreign sub pays BUY-IN to US sub for existing IP value
view.s367d.csa.financial_substance: Each party must contribute substantial cost + receive substantial benefit
view.s367d.csa.ongoing: Ongoing R&D costs SHARED in proportion to expected benefits
view.s367d.csa.platform_contribution: Platform Contribution: existing IP buy-in determined via Income Method / CUT / DCM
view.s367d.csa.purpose: Avoid § 367(d) by SHARING development costs of new IP
view.s367d.csa.stock_based: Stock-based compensation MUST be included in cost base (Altera v. Comm'r)
view.s367d.csa_note: CSA in place: § 367(d) deemed royalty AVOIDED. Buy-in payment + ongoing cost share. Critical: stock-based compensation MUST be included in cost base (Altera). Document IP development costs + benefit allocation contemporaneously. Tax Court has reviewed Amazon + Altera challenges to enforce arm's-length CSA pricing.
view.s367d.def.goodwill: Goodwill and going concern value — TCJA NEW
view.s367d.def.licenses: Licenses, permits, other rights granted by governmental units
view.s367d.def.other: Methods of doing business, business records, operating systems
view.s367d.def.patents: Patents, inventions, formulas, processes, designs, patterns, knowhow
view.s367d.def.trademarks: Trademarks, trade names, brand names, franchises
view.s367d.h1.title: // § 367(d) IP OUTBOUND
view.s367d.hint.intro: US transferor of INTANGIBLE PROPERTY to foreign corp must include ANNUAL DEEMED ROYALTY over IP's useful life. TCJA 2017 expansion: 'intangible' now includes workforce in place, goodwill, going concern, customer lists. Royalty = arm's-length annual amount (parallel to § 482 transfer pricing). Cost Sharing Arrangement (CSA) § 482 alternative — buy-in + ongoing R&D shares. 2017 effective: applies post-Dec 31 2017 transfers. § 6038B reporting. Avoid: keep IP in US, or CSA, or royalty structure (not outbound transfer).
view.s368.h1.title: // § 368 REORGANIZATIONS
view.s368.hint.intro: Seven types of tax-free reorganizations § 368(a)(1)(A)-(G). Common requirements: Continuity of Interest (40%+ stock consideration), Continuity of Business Enterprise, Business Purpose, Plan of Reorganization. Carry over: § 381 sub attributes (NOL, E&P, credits). § 382 limitation on NOL post-ownership-change. Boot = non-stock consideration recognized as gain (not loss). Form 8806 filed for B / C / D acquisitive reorgs.
view.s368.jd.business_purpose: Business Purpose: corp-level (not solely tax-motivated). Gregory v. Helvering (1935)
view.s368.jd.cobe: Continuity of Business Enterprise (COBE): continue HISTORIC business OR use HISTORIC assets in business
view.s368.jd.coi: Continuity of Interest (COI): 40%+ stock consideration; measured at time of binding agreement (post-2005)
view.s368.jd.economic_substance: § 7701(o) codified economic substance doctrine — meaningful change required + non-tax purpose
view.s368.jd.plan: Plan of Reorganization: formal corporate action documenting steps
view.s368.jd.step_transaction: Step transaction: integrated steps treated as one (Smith v. Comm'r, Walden v. Comm'r)
view.s368.jd.substance_form: Substance over form: tax follows economic substance
view.s368.no_qualify_note: Reorg requirements NOT met → TAXABLE transaction. Full target gain recognized at corporate level (20%+ LTCG) + shareholder level (sale treatment) → double tax.
view.s368.s382.bankruptcy_exception: § 382(l)(5): G reorg in bankruptcy — historic creditors as continuing shareholders
view.s368.s382.built_in_gain: RBIG (recognized built-in gain) adjustments increase limit during 5-yr period
view.s368.s382.limit: Annual NOL limit: target value × federal long-term tax-exempt rate (~4-5%)
view.s368.s382.notice2003_65: Notice 2003-65 + 2019 proposed regs detail built-in gain measurement
view.s368.s382.trigger: Ownership change: > 50 pct-point shift over 3-yr test period among 5% shareholders
view.s36b.h1.title: // § 36B ACA PREMIUM TAX CREDIT
view.s36b.hint.intro: Refundable credit to reduce monthly health insurance premiums on Healthcare.gov. Pre-IRA: available only 100-400% FPL (cliff). ARPA / IRA 2022-2025: extended to ALL incomes when premium > 8.5% of MAGI. Reconciled on Form 8962. Critical for self-employed traders — losing one year's APTC due to high gain costs thousands. 2026 sunset: 400% FPL cliff returns.
view.s36b.plan.advance: If you'll exceed advance estimate, ACT NOW to drop MAGI
view.s36b.plan.cap_repayment: Pre-ARPA: repayment cap 200% FPL — limit repayment risk
view.s36b.plan.cliff_2026: 2026 cliff returns: get below 400% FPL or lose entire subsidy
view.s36b.plan.harvesting: Loss harvest to offset realized gains pre-year-end
view.s36b.plan.roth_timing: Time Roth conversions for low-income years
view.s36b.plan.solo_401k: Solo 401(k) max contribution to drop MAGI below cliff
view.s36b.warning.repay: Advance PTC exceeds earned PTC → you OWE the difference back on Form 8962. Repayment caps apply < 400% FPL. ≥ 400% FPL (pre-IRA) = full repayment.
view.s38.carry_note: Unused credit carries back 1 yr (file Form 1045 / 1139 quick refund) OR forward 20 yrs. For IRA 2022 specified credits: consider § 6417 direct pay (tax-exempts) or § 6418 transferability (taxables) to monetize immediately at 88-95¢ on $1.
view.s38.comp.alcohol: § 40 Alcohol fuel mixtures + § 40A biodiesel + § 40B Sustainable Aviation Fuel
view.s38.comp.employer: § 45E Small Employer Pension Setup + § 45F Childcare + § 45R Health Insurance
view.s38.comp.investment: Investment credit § 46: § 48 ITC, § 48C, § 48E, § 47 rehab, § 48D semi mfg
view.s38.comp.renewable: § 45 Renewable Electricity PTC + § 45Y Clean Electricity PTC
view.s38.comp.research: § 41 Research credit (incremental QRE method or ASC)
view.s38.comp.work_opportunity: § 51 Work Opportunity Tax Credit (WOTC): up to $9,600 per qualified hire
view.s38.h1.title: // § 38 GENERAL BUSINESS CREDIT
view.s38.hint.intro: Aggregator of business credits: § 41 R&D + § 45 PTC + § 48 ITC + § 45Q + § 30D + § 45W + § 45L + § 45X + Empowerment Zone + WOTC + Disabled Access + many more. Limit: net regular tax above TMT (Tentative Minimum Tax) + 25% of next $25K. Carryback 1-yr, Carryforward 20-yr (§ 39). Forms 3800 + 8000-series source forms. § 6417 direct pay + § 6418 transferability for IRA 2022 specified credits (different rules per credit).
view.s38.lim.25pct: + 25% of remaining tax above $25K (after TMT reached)
view.s38.lim.basic: Limit = Net Tax (regular) above Tentative Minimum Tax (TMT)
view.s38.lim.carry_back: Unused: carry back 1 yr first, then 20-year carryforward
view.s38.lim.eligible_small: Eligible small biz: R&D + energy + various credits can offset AMT entirely
view.s38.lim.pre_acquisition: § 39(d) pre-acquisition GBCs: SRLY-limited similar to NOL
view.s38.lim.s39_expiration: § 39 carryforward expires after 20 yrs → permanent loss
view.s38.lim.specified: 'Specified credits' (R&D portion, energy ITC, etc.) can offset AMT + TMT
view.s382.h1.title: // § 382 NOL LIMITATION (OWNERSHIP CHANGE)
view.s382.hint.intro: Limits NOL utilization after ownership change to annual limit = FMV × LT tax-exempt rate. § 382(h) RBIG/RBIL 5-yr. § 382(c) COBE 2-yr or NOLs eliminated.
view.s401a9.ex.first_year: First-year RMD can be delayed to April 1 of following year (but creates double RMD year)
view.s401a9.ex.inherited: Inherited IRA: separate rules (10-yr rule + EDB exceptions)
view.s401a9.ex.qcd_offset: QCD up to $105k offsets RMD income inclusion
view.s401a9.ex.roth_ira: Roth IRA: NO RMD during owner's life (SECURE 2.0 eliminated Roth 401(k) RMD too)
view.s401a9.ex.still_working: Still working + < 5% owner: 401(k) RMD deferred at current employer
view.s401a9.ex.terminal: Terminal illness: SECURE 2.0 exception (lifetime expectancy < 7 yrs)
view.s401a9.h1.title: // § 401(a)(9) RMD CALCULATOR
view.s401a9.hint.intro: SECURE Act 2019 + SECURE 2.0 2022 changed RBD (Required Beginning Date): Born ≤ 6/30/1949: age 70½. Born 7/1/1949 → 12/31/1950: age 72. Born 1951-1959: age 73. Born 1960+: age 75. RMD = prior-year Dec 31 balance ÷ life-expectancy divisor (Uniform Lifetime Table). QCD up to $105,000 (2024) from IRA offsets RMD without inclusion in income. Penalty for missed RMD: 25% (10% if timely corrected).
view.s401a9.note.joint: Spouse > 10 yrs younger sole beneficiary: use Joint Life Expectancy Table (longer divisor → smaller RMD). Recalculate annually.
view.s401a9.qcd.501c3: Direct transfer to 501(c)(3) public charity only (not DAF, foundation)
view.s401a9.qcd.cgi: Counts toward RMD if RMD age
view.s401a9.qcd.cgi_2024: $105,000 limit 2024 (indexed); SECURE 2.0 split $50k one-time CRT / CGA
view.s401a9.qcd.medicare: Lower AGI reduces Medicare IRMAA premiums (2-yr lookback)
view.s401a9.qcd.muni: Lower AGI keeps muni bond / Social Security from being taxed
view.s401a9.qcd.no_agi: Excludes amount from AGI — better than itemized charitable deduction
view.s401a9.qcd.over_70: Available age 70½+ even if RMD not yet required
view.s401a9.qcd.spouse: Each spouse has separate limit if separate IRAs
view.s401h.doc.affirmation: Post-2020 SECURE Act: employee self-certification accepted instead of docs
view.s401h.doc.funeral: Funeral: itemized funeral bills + death certificate
view.s401h.doc.home: Home purchase: P&S agreement, closing statement
view.s401h.doc.medical: Medical: itemized bills, doctor's letter, insurance EOBs
view.s401h.ex.domestic_abuse: Domestic abuse victim (lesser of $10k or 50% balance)
view.s401h.h1.title: // § 401(k) HARDSHIP WITHDRAWAL
view.s401h.hint.intro: 'Immediate + heavy financial need' + 'necessary' + 'no other resources'. Safe-harbor categories: medical, principal residence purchase, post-secondary education, prevent eviction / foreclosure, funeral, principal residence damage. SECURE 2.0 improvements: Roth source allowed + $1k emergency / yr + terminal illness + domestic abuse + disaster relief + employer match + own contributions all eligible.
view.s408a.h1.title: // § 408A ROTH IRA
view.s408a.hint.intro: After-tax contributions + tax-free qualified distributions. 2024 limits $7K / $8K catch-up. MAGI phase-out $146-161K single / $230-240K MFJ. Backdoor + mega backdoor.
view.s408d3.h1.title: // § 408(d)(3) BOBROW IRA ROLLOVER
view.s408d3.hint.intro: Bobrow v. Comm'r (2014): one indirect rollover per 12 months applies PER TAXPAYER across ALL your IRAs (Traditional + Roth + SEP + SIMPLE). Pre-Bobrow misreading allowed per-account. Second indirect rollover within 12 mo: amount taxable + 10% penalty if < 59½ + excess contribution + 6% excise. Trustee-to-trustee transfers do NOT count — unlimited. Roth conversions don't count.
view.s408d3.warning.violation: Multiple indirect rollovers within 12 months — second + later treated as TAXABLE DISTRIBUTION + possible 10% penalty + 6% excess contribution excise. File Form 5329 + consider self-certification.
view.s408d3.work.60_days: If you do indirect: 60 days strict; banks must withhold 20% (less for IRA)
view.s408d3.work.conversions: Roth conversions don't count toward the limit
view.s408d3.work.direct: Always do direct trustee-to-trustee transfer — unlimited per year
view.s408d3.work.private_letter: PLR for other reasons — $10k+ user fee
view.s408d3.work.self_certify: Late rollover relief: Rev. Proc. 2016-47 self-certification (11 reasons)
view.s408d3.work.spousal_separate: Spouse's IRA rollovers count separately (each spouse has own limit)
view.s409A.dist.cic: Change in control — § 409A specific definitions (not always M&A)
view.s409A.dist.emergency: Unforeseeable emergency — limited; no medical / casualty insurance
view.s409A.dist.specified_employee: SPECIFIED EMPLOYEE (public co): 6-month delay on separation distributions
view.s409A.dist.time: Specified time or schedule — must be objectively determinable
view.s409A.exc.contractor: Non-employee director / contractor with multiple unrelated customers
view.s409A.exc.foreign: Foreign plans of bona fide foreign nationals (limited carve-out)
view.s409A.exc.separation_safe_harbor: Separation pay: 2-yr cap + 2× FICA wages limit ($330K × 2 = $660K)
view.s409A.exc.short_term: Short-term deferral: paid within 2.5 months of vesting
view.s409A.exc.stock_rights: Stock rights: at-the-money options + RSUs (settled when vest)
view.s409A.exc.welfare: Welfare benefit plans (group health, life, disability)
view.s409A.h1.title: // § 409A NONQUAL DEFERRED COMP
view.s409A.hint.intro: Defers tax on comp not actually / constructively received. VIOLATION: ALL deferred amounts taxed in current year + 20% additional tax + underpayment interest at 1% above market rate. 6 permitted distribution events: separation, disability, death, time/schedule, change in control, unforeseeable emergency. Initial election: 30 days post-eligibility OR before year start. Subsequent deferrals: 1-yr advance + 5-yr push-out. Specified employee (public co): 6-month delay on separation pay. Form W-2 box 12 code Y/Z.
view.s409A.pen.aggregation: Aggregation rules: all plans of same type aggregated for violation
view.s409A.pen.all_deferred: ALL deferred amounts taxable in year of violation (not just current vested)
view.s409A.pen.amended_return: Amended return required for corrected violations
view.s409A.pen.california: California: 20% state + interest stacks on top (parallel state law)
view.s409A.pen.correction: Notice 2010-6 + 2008-113: limited correction safe harbors (some violations)
view.s409A.pen.interest: + Interest at underpayment rate + 1% from year of deferral
view.s409A.pen.no_employee_relief: No relief for employee even if employer's fault
view.s409A.viol.acceleration: Acceleration of distribution to event other than 6 permitted
view.s409A.viol.cic_definition: Inaccurate change in control definition (must match § 409A regs)
view.s409A.viol.discount: Discounted stock options: strike < FMV = built-in deferred comp
view.s409A.viol.late_change: Late change of distribution timing without 1-yr advance + 5-yr push-out
view.s409A.viol.no_election: Failure to make timely initial election
view.s409A.viol.no_six_month: Specified employee separation pay distributed before 6-month delay
view.s409A.viol.severance: Severance > safe harbor without 6-month delay (public co)
view.s409A.violation_note: § 409A violation triggers ALL deferred amounts current taxation + 20% penalty + interest. Effective tax rate often EXCEEDS 60%. Consider Notice 2010-6 / 2008-113 correction safe harbors for operational failures. Document failure prevention via plan administration audit.
view.s41.h1.title: // § 41 RESEARCH CREDIT
view.s41.hint.intro: Regular method: 20% × (Current QREs − Base Amount). Base = avg gross receipts × fixed base %. ASC § 41(c)(5): 14% × (Current QREs − 50% × avg prior 3 yrs). Payroll tax election: qualified small biz (≤ $5M current revenue + ≤ 5 yrs revenue) uses up to $500K credit against employer FICA (PATH Act 2015). QREs: wages + supplies + contract research (65%) + computer time-share. § 174: 2022+ mandatory 5-yr / 15-yr amortization replaces immediate expensing.
view.s41.qre.basic_research: Basic research: separate § 41(b)(3) treatment — credit at 20% direct
view.s41.qre.computer: Computer time-share: cost of cloud computing + hosted services for qualified research
view.s41.qre.contract: Contract research: 65% of payments to contractors (75% for qualified energy research consortium)
view.s41.qre.no_capital: Cost of fixed assets NOT QRE — depreciation already deducted under § 168
view.s41.qre.no_funded: Funded research (paid by 3rd party) NOT QRE for performer
view.s41.qre.no_overhead: Overhead, indirect costs, depreciation NOT QRE
view.s41.qre.supplies: Supplies: tangible property used in research (NOT capital assets > 1 yr)
view.s41.qre.wages: Wages: W-2 Box 1 of employees engaged in qualified research (direct + supervision + support)
view.s41.qsb_note: Qualified small business: up to $500K of credit applied against employer FICA (6.2% OASDI portion) via Form 8974 + Form 941 quarterly. Valuable for pre-revenue startups with no income tax liability. Carryforward 5 years.
view.s41.s280c.basic: § 280C(c): deduction reduced by credit amount (no double benefit)
view.s41.s280c.bill_pending: Various proposals (TCDTRA, etc.) would restore § 174 immediate expensing
view.s41.s280c.coordination: Coordinate with § 174: § 280C applies to current QRE, but § 174 amortization spreads
view.s41.s280c.reduced: § 280C(c)(2) reduced credit election: take 79% credit but no deduction reduction (21%-rate)
view.s41.s280c.tcja: TCJA pre-2022: § 174 immediate expense; post-2022: forced amortization → credit value rises relative to deduction
view.s41.tst.all_four: ALL FOUR required — fact-intensive determination, contemporaneous documentation critical
view.s41.tst.documentation: Document: project objectives, technical challenges, alternatives evaluated, test results, time tracking
view.s41.tst.exclusions: Exclusions § 41(d)(4): post-commercial production, adaptation, duplication, surveys, foreign research, software for internal use (3-part test)
view.s41.tst.experimentation: PROCESS OF EXPERIMENTATION: evaluate alternatives via systematic process (hypothesis → test → analyze)
view.s41.tst.permitted_purpose: PERMITTED PURPOSE: new / improved business component (function, performance, reliability, quality)
view.s41.tst.tech_uncertainty: TECHNOLOGICAL UNCERTAINTY: develop info to eliminate uncertainty about capability / method / design
view.s41.tst.technological: TECHNOLOGICAL IN NATURE: rely on hard sciences (engineering, physics, biology, computer science, chemistry)
view.s412.at_risk_note: AT-RISK STATUS: § 430 load factor adds 40% to target normal cost. § 436 benefit restrictions: no lump sums (< 80%); no future accruals (< 60%); 50% lump sum available at 60-80%. Notify participants. Address shortfall via: increase contributions, plan freezes, benefit formula reduction (no clawback of accrued).
view.s412.atr.benefit_restrictions: § 436 benefit restrictions: no lump sums, no accruals if < 60% funded
view.s412.atr.computation: At-risk computation: accelerated assumptions + load factor
view.s412.atr.continue: Continued at-risk: 3 consecutive years → 'at-risk' load factor permanently
view.s412.atr.exemption: Recent shortfall exemption: temporary relief in plan years 2014-2024 (extension acts)
view.s412.atr.notice: Notice to participants: required when in at-risk + benefit restrictions
